currentFocus: Documentation/state are aligned for the authorized v1.0.9 portable ZIP release slice. Current docs promote the first-time-user UX/code-flow safety repairs into the next unsigned WinUI ZIP release while preserving no-signing/no-installer/no-gameplay/no-online/no-rebuild/no-original-BEA-mutation boundaries.

currentDocsTruth.wave1220StaticCloseout: Wave1220 static closeout acceptance: active current-risk focused accounting is `1179/1179 = 100.00%`; remaining active focused work: 0
currentDocsTruth.preSolUltraQualityWave: Current tracked changes are bounded source/docs/tooling hygiene for AppCore save analysis, Lore path containment, ZIP extraction safety, validation-tool behavior, release-profile freshness, private-path redaction checks, and concise baton state. They are not release, runtime, live-Ghidra, gameplay, or online-readiness evidence.
currentDocsTruth.releaseDocs: v1.0.9 is the authorized next public GitHub Release slice. It publishes OnslaughtToolkit-winui-v1.0.9-win-x64.zip and its SHA-256 sidecar, with 949 Lore documents, friendly root layout, passing launch/Home/Lore/representative Media smokes, and process cleanup. v1.0.8 remains historical. `release/readiness/winui_zip_release_v1_0_9_2026-07-05.md` records the release candidate facts; `release/readiness/winui_action_reaction_map_v1_0_9_2026-07-05.md` records harder release questions plus a source-derived WinUI handler-family action/reaction map; `release/readiness/winui_control_action_matrix_v1_0_9_2026-07-05.tsv` records the per-control source matrix. Do not promote signing, installer/MSIX/Store, announcement, gameplay proof, online capability, rebuild parity, visual parity, or installed-game/original BEA.exe mutation.
currentDocsTruth.lore: Lore docs and mirrored roadmap material should remain public-safe and source/tooling scoped. Lore pack and browser hardening is traversal/path-boundary support, not a full filesystem proof or runtime UI proof.
currentDocsTruth.patchBenchAndWinUI: WinUI copy and metrics surfaces are source/static or local-build aids unless native UIA or visual proof is freshly run and recorded. PatchBench remains disabled/safety-scoped where runtime proof is absent.
currentDocsTruth.reAndRebuild: Static RE closeout and rebuild planning docs remain planning and static-accounting surfaces. They are not runtime parser proof, visual/gameplay proof, live Ghidra read-back, generated asset output, or rebuild parity.

hardPayloadBoundary: Tracked docs/state must not add hard payloads, secrets, raw local campaign paths, raw storage manifests, private proof paths, copied executables, screenshots/frame dumps, full Ghidra databases, .env files, build output, package output, or runtime cache material.

remainingGatePlan: Commit and push the v1.0.9 release source update.
Publish the v1.0.9 GitHub Release with the validated ZIP and SHA-256 sidecar.
Keep signing, installer/MSIX/Store work, announcement, gameplay proof, online capability, rebuild parity, visual parity, and installed-game/original BEA.exe mutation out of scope.

nextSteps: Next UX hardening, if authorized, should focus on Asset Library first-run guidance, Windowed & Mods density, and replacing any remaining dense maintainer phrasing with plain user flow text.
Keep historical v1.0.7 release notes intact as historical records.
Leave any future signing, installer, announcement, or stronger proof work to a separate authorized slice.
